injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Expected available for the tournament; club monitoring

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Substituted off after 69 minutes as a precaution in a 2-2 draw; walked off unaided

injuryUpdates.source: Flashscore injury history (entry from 11.05.2026); RotoWire / Burnley Express via theScore.com (15 May 2026)
injuryUpdates.notes: Recurrence worry after the late-March hamstring strain that already ruled him out of the Haiti and Canada friendlies. Interim Burnley coach Mike Jackson downplayed severity. Selected in the 26-man squad announced 15 May 2026, so currently expected to be fit for the opener on 14 June, but he is the highest-profile fitness watch in the squad.

squadChanges.details: Officially named captain in Lamouchi's 26-man squad announced 15 May 2026, replacing 101-cap Ferjani Sassi, who was omitted. Eintracht Frankfurt defensive midfielder is now the on-pitch leader.
squadChanges.source: Bulinews / Africa Top Sports / Heavy.com (15 May 2026)

squadChanges.details: Union Berlin midfielder, previously a first-choice starter and listed as a star player, was left out of the final 26-man squad announced 15 May 2026. A genuine selection surprise; he had been widely tipped to feature alongside Skhiri and Mejbri.
squadChanges.source: ysscores.com official squad list; Heavy.com squad breakdown (15 May 2026)

squadChanges.details: Top qualification goalscorer (4 goals, including the 94th-minute winner vs Equatorial Guinea that sealed qualification) omitted from the final 26-man squad. Major surprise omission.
Previous captain and 101-cap veteran cut from the 26-man squad. Marks a generational shift under Lamouchi.
95-cap veteran defender cut from the final squad.

Union Berlin defensive midfielder (brother of 2014 winner Sami Khedira) handed first international call-up at age 32 after completing naturalisation in March. Adds Bundesliga experience to the midfield.

19-year-old Karlsruhe attacker who debuted in the Haiti/Canada friendlies was omitted after his father phoned Lamouchi to say it was too early for his son to attend the tournament; Ben Farhat did not return the coach's follow-up call.

bettingOddsUpdate.source: Aggregated from UK bookmakers (Bet365, Betway, Betfred, Coral) via freebets.com and squawka.com, May 2026
bettingOddsUpdate.notes: Tunisia priced as the clear Group F outsider. Group elimination at 4/6 is the favoured stage of exit; advancing from the group is roughly even money at 21/20. Outright winner +50000 (500/1).

coachUpdate.notes: Lamouchi (in post since March 2026) named his 26-man squad on 15 May 2026 and has scheduled Austria (1 June, Vienna) and Belgium (6 June, Brussels) as pre-tournament friendlies.

summary: Tunisia's 26-man squad, announced 15 May 2026, is the dominant development of the window: Ellyes Skhiri is formally captain, while Aissa Laidouni, top qualifying scorer Mohamed Ali Ben Romdhane, ex-captain Ferjani Sassi and veteran Yassine Meriah were all cut, marking a deliberate generational reset by coach Sabri Lamouchi. The chief fitness watch is Hannibal Mejbri, who was substituted off as a precaution in Burnley's 10 May draw with Aston Villa and is currently flagged with a hamstring entry, though he made the squad and is expected to be available. No competitive or friendly matches have been played since 16 April; warm-ups against Austria (1 June) and Belgium (6 June) are still to come.
